# Instagram Post — OpenAI Partners with Accenture, BCG, McKinsey, and Capgemini in Enterprise Push
# Week: Week of 2026-02-23
# Brand Voice: GYanTech AI — Visionary, executive audience, lead generation
# Status: Review before posting
# Auto-generated: 2026-03-02T18:47:02.345Z

---

OpenAI just brought McKinsey, BCG, and Accenture to the table.

This week, OpenAI announced multiyear partnerships with four of the world's largest consulting firms — Accenture, Boston Consulting Group, Capgemini, and McKinsey. Their job: help enterprise clients define and execute AI strategy using OpenAI's platform.

OpenAI's CFO Sarah Friar confirmed enterprise already makes up about 40% of revenue, with 50% targeted by year-end. Meanwhile, Anthropic has made similar moves with Deloitte and Accenture.

Here's what this really means. The AI model providers are no longer just selling technology — they're selling transformation. And they're using the consultants who already have your CEO's ear to do it.

For business leaders, this changes the dynamics. Strategy conversations about AI will increasingly come pre-packaged with a vendor preference built in. The companies that maintain strategic independence — choosing tools based on their own objectives rather than a partner's incentives — will be the ones that extract real, lasting value.

AI strategy should serve your business. Not the other way around.

GYanTech AI builds vendor-neutral AI strategies for executive teams. Link in bio.

## Fact-check notes
- OpenAI entered multiyear partnerships with Accenture, Boston Consulting Group, Capgemini, and McKinsey & Co. — CNBC, Feb 23, 2026
- OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar stated enterprises account for roughly 40% of OpenAI's business, with expectations to reach ~50% by end of year — CNBC, Feb 23, 2026
- Anthropic has also inked deals with consulting giants including Deloitte and Accenture — TechCrunch, Feb 23, 2026
